title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine built on sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the complete system through an interactive Textual terminal UI with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices, producing auditable, evidence-backed hiring recommendations exportable as structured reports.

title: Portfolio
description: Built a personal portfolio website to showcase data science projects, experience, and a resume, deployed as a static site at nathansso.github.io/portfolio. Architected the site using vanilla HTML, CSS, and ES module JavaScript with a single source-of-truth data layer (`data/site.js`) that drives all four pages simultaneously, eliminating redundancy without relying on any framework or build tool. Automated GitHub repository metadata syncing via custom Node.js scripts (`sync-projects.js`, `merge-projects.js`) that fetch live repo data and merge it into the data layer while preserving manually authored fields like project blurbs and categories.

title: Diginetica: GNN vs. Flat Ranker for Session Recommendation
description: Built an end-to-end relational deep learning pipeline on the Diginetica (CIKM Cup 2016) e-commerce logs, using RelBench to convert the relational database into a heterogeneous graph — 8 node types (items, sessions, users plus view/query/click/queried-item/purchase events) and 22 edge types over 11 foreign-key relations. Engineered an `abs_time` event clock at microsecond resolution by folding intra-session millisecond offsets into the event date, enabling time-aware neighbor sampling that admits only neighbors strictly earlier than a per-row seed time so the receptive field never peeks at the future. Defined two tasks: session-conversion entity classification (binary, ~4% positive rate, AUPRC/AUROC) and next-item link prediction via intra-session leave-last-out on 218K sessions. The GNN is a two-tower heterogeneous GraphSAGE encoder (HeteroEncoder + temporal encoder) splitting into session and item towers scored by dot product, trained with sampled-softmax over in-batch and pool negatives. It is compared against an XGBoost LambdaRank ranker (`rank:ndcg`) over anchor-based 1-hop features — co-view, co-purchase, transition, category, popularity, price, is_repeat. A shared per-row candidate pool (co-view + transition + co-purchase + category + popularity backfill, built on train statistics only) lets both models rank the identical set, isolating ranking quality from candidate recall, with Optuna sweeps (persistent SQLite, median pruning) tuning both on Novel Recall@10. Evaluation decomposes Recall@10, NDCG@10, and MRR across slices — Repeat/Novel, popularity (Head/Torso/Tail), and session length — through a single masked `task.evaluate` code path, alongside structural ablations on hop depth, item-feature capacity, and scoring head (pooled dot vs. maxpool vs. ColBERT-style late interaction).

title: Idx_cont_training
description: Built an end-to-end MLOps pipeline for continuously training machine learning models on real estate listing data, incorporating automated data streaming, preprocessing, and model retraining workflows. The pipeline integrated geospatial feature engineering with GeoPandas, gradient boosting models (LightGBM and XGBoost), and custom utility modules for outlier detection and missingness handling to maintain model accuracy as new listing data arrived. Scikit-learn was used to orchestrate preprocessing and model evaluation, with Joblib enabling efficient model serialization and deployment across retraining cycles.

title: Portfolio Live Editor
description: Built an Express.js proxy server that intercepted and modified HTTP responses from a portfolio site, injecting a WYSIWYG editing overlay into every served page. The overlay communicated with a custom REST API to read and write changes directly to the source data file, persisting updates instantly without any build pipeline or recompilation. This replaced manual file editing with a point-and-click interface layered over the live site, eliminating context-switching during content iteration.

title: Real Time ML Feature Store And Quant Research Platform
description: Built a modular quantitative research platform featuring a real-time ML feature store, event-driven backtesting engine, and tax-loss harvesting rules engine using Python, DuckDB, and Feast. The system ingested daily OHLCV data via yfinance, stored features in Parquet with a SQL query layer over DuckDB, and applied wash-sale-aware harvesting logic on top of lot-level position tracking. A mean-reversion classifier served as an ML gate for harvest decisions, integrating Feast-managed features directly into the backtesting pipeline.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on historical real estate data with features such as location, square footage, and property characteristics. Developed an interactive Streamlit interface with dynamic search functionality and Plotly visualizations to allow users to input property details and receive price predictions in real time. The pipeline incorporated scikit-learn preprocessing, feature engineering, and model serialization via Joblib to deliver a production-ready forecasting tool deployable as a standalone web application.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to maximize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving approximately 90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, uncovering conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science project using Python to collect, process, and visualize structured datasets through HTTP requests and pandas-based data manipulation. Applied statistical analysis and generated visualizations with matplotlib and seaborn to uncover patterns and trends within the data. Demonstrated proficiency in the full data science workflow, from raw data ingestion to insight communication through compelling visual outputs.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, hands-on implementation of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, NumPy, and Pandas to process and evaluate network or behavioral data through custom-coded logic rather than automated pipelines. Leveraged the Requests library to fetch external datasets and applied structured data manipulation techniques to identify dormant or latent patterns within the data. The project served as a ground-up notebook-based prototype, providing interpretable step-by-step visibility into the detection methodology.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack fantasy football roster optimization tool that integrates with the Sleeper API to surface real-time league data, roster compositions, and available player pools through a React + Vite frontend. Developed a Python-based AI assistant using pandas and NumPy to process player statistics and generate data-driven roster recommendations. The system combined live API data ingestion with algorithmic analysis to provide actionable lineup decisions directly within the browser interface.

title: UCSD Research Lab
description: Conducted research at UCSD under Professor Andre Gray, applying statistical modeling and machine learning techniques using Python (Jupyter Notebook) and R to investigate research questions in the lab's domain. Developed and iterated on data pipelines, visualizations, and quantitative analyses to support ongoing experimental work. Contributed to academic research by translating raw datasets into reproducible, interpretable findings aligned with the lab's scientific objectives.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML. Mapped bike lane usage and traffic volume data onto a dynamic interface, allowing users to filter and examine cycling activity across different routes and locations. The project highlighted key high-traffic corridors and underserved areas within the city's bike infrastructure network.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This introductory project demonstrated the core GitHub Desktop workflow, including initializing a repository, editing a README file, and committing changes through the desktop interface. The exercise involved direct file editing and version control tracking using GitHub Desktop on a local machine. The project established foundational familiarity with Git-based project management and documentation practices.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
